title: Amauropelta supranitens
intro: Amauropelta supranitens is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, commonly known as the mountain maiden fern. The health benefits of Amauropelta supranitens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in folk medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Amauropelta supranitens is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Amauropelta supranitens is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the maidenhair ferns. This species belongs to the genus Amauropelta, which is a group of ferns characterized by their simple leaves and small spore-bearing structures. The classification of Amauropelta supranitens is based on its morphological characteristics, such as the shape and arrangement of its leaves and the structure of its spores. The species is also part of the order Polypodiales, which includes a wide range of ferns with similar characteristics. Additionally, Amauropelta supranitens can be further classified as a member of the subclass Polypodiidae.
